informal_statement.text: Given an unweighted directed graph represented by an adjacency list of n vertices and a source vertex, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is Some(k) if the shortest path from source to v uses k edges, and None if v is unreachable.
preconditions: every neighbor index is in [0, n)
required_properties: if dist[v] == Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges
if dist[v] == Some(k), no directed path from source to v uses fewer than k edges
if dist[v] == None, there is no directed path from source to v
edge_cases: graph with a single vertex and no edges

semantic_units.description: Length invariant: dist has length n, equal to the number of vertices.
common_failure_modes: stating an upper bound instead of equality
forgetting to anchor the source distance

semantic_units.description: Reachability witness: if dist[v] = Some(k), there is a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges.
common_failure_modes: stating the weaker 'v is reachable' without length

semantic_units.description: Minimality: if dist[v] = Some(k), no path uses fewer than k edges.
common_failure_modes: confusing the minimal and any-path formulations

semantic_units.description: Unreachability: dist[v] = None iff no path exists from source to v.
common_failure_modes: only stating one direction of the iff
conflating 'no path' with 'path of length > bound'

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output has the same length as the number of vertices.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src : Nat), src < adj.length → (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? src = some (some 0)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is zero.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∃ path : List Nat, path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v ∧ path.length = k + 1
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), there is a path from source to v with exactly k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v k : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some (some k) → ∀ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src → path.getLast? = some v → k + 1 ≤ path.length
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), no path from source to v has fewer than k edges.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (adj : Adj) (src v : Nat), (bfsShortestPath adj src).get? v = some none ↔ ¬ ∃ (path : List Nat), path.head? = some src ∧ path.getLast? = some v
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: dist[v] = None if and only if no path from source to v exists.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_length (adj : Adj) (source : Nat) : (bfsShortestPath adj source).length = adj.length := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance table has the same length as the adjacency list (aligned with the number of vertices).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_source_anchor (adj : Adj) (source : Nat) :
  (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? source = some (some 0) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is exactly 0 edges.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_reachability_witness (adj : Adj) (source v k : Nat)
    (hv : v < adj.length)
    (hk : (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some (some k)) :
    ∃ path : List Nat,
      path.length = k + 1 ∧
      path.head? = some source ∧
      path.get? k = some v ∧
      (∀ i : Nat, i < k →
        let a := path.get? i
        let b := path.get? (i+1)
        a.isSome ∧ b.isSome ∧
        match a, b with
        | some ai, some bi => bi ∈ (adj.get? ai).getD []
        | _, _ => False) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), then there exists a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges (k+1 vertices).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_minimality (adj : Adj) (source v k : Nat)
    (hv : v < adj.length)
    (hk : (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some (some k)) :
    ¬ ∃ k' : Nat,
      k' < k ∧
      (∃ path : List Nat,
        path.length = k' + 1 ∧
        path.head? = some source ∧
        path.get? k' = some v ∧
        (∀ i : Nat, i < k' →
          let a := path.get? i
          let b := path.get? (i+1)
          a.isSome ∧ b.isSome ∧
          match a, b with
          | some ai, some bi => bi ∈ (adj.get? ai).getD []
          | _, _ => False)) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), then there is no directed path from source to v using fewer than k edges.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_unreachability_iff (adj : Adj) (source v : Nat)
    (hv : v < adj.length) :
    (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some none ↔
      ¬ ∃ k : Nat,
        ∃ path : List Nat,
          path.length = k + 1 ∧
          path.head? = some source ∧
          path.get? k = some v ∧
          (∀ i : Nat, i < k →
            let a := path.get? i
            let b := path.get? (i+1)
            a.isSome ∧ b.isSome ∧
            match a, b with
            | some ai, some bi => bi ∈ (adj.get? ai).getD []
            | _, _ => False) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: dist[v] is None exactly when there is no directed path from source to v.
